task_completion.description: LibreCAD application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD graphical user interface is open on the screen, showing the drawing area, toolbars, and panels.

task_completion.description: Layer '0' is selected as the active drawing layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Layer List panel (typically on the right side of the LibreCAD window), the layer named '0' is highlighted or selected, indicating it is the active layer.

task_completion.description: A rectangle is drawn in the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangular shape is visible in the main drawing area of LibreCAD.

task_completion.description: The rectangle is drawn with the bottom-left corner at (0, 0) and top-right corner at (3000, 2000).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the command line history at the bottom of the LibreCAD window for entries like '0,0' and '3000,2000' following a rectangle command. Alternatively, verify the rectangle's position on the grid aligns with the origin (0,0) and extends to 3000 on the X-axis and 2000 on the Y-axis.

The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'rectangle_task.dxf'. Alternatively, a 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'rectangle_task.dxf' in the '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/' directory, or a terminal/file manager window confirms the file's creation at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the LibreCAD GUI to draw the rectangle.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking on LibreCAD drawing tools (like the Rectangle tool) or typing commands into the LibreCAD command line, rather than using a Python script or terminal command to generate the DXF file.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write or edit the DXF file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) open with raw DXF markup being manually typed or pasted.

integrity.description: The file was saved using LibreCAD's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action should be visible via the LibreCAD 'File -> Save As' menu, save dialog box, or LibreCAD command line, rather than just moving/renaming a pre-existing file in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The environment may contain a real 2-car garage floor plan DXF file (floorplan.dxf, 1,117,143 bytes, AutoCAD DXF v2004) with 24 named layers (e.g., 'A-WALL', 'A-CASE-1'), 624 lines, 63 dimensions, and 98 text elements. While this file is present in the environment (under an MIT license, despite script claims of Apache), the agent's primary objective is strictly to draw the specified rectangle on layer '0' and save it as rectangle_task.dxf.
